what_it_does: This file defines a first-class registry row for the dragnet stage-2 fine extraction role: it imports the Fine schema and FINE_PROMPT from contracts.dragnet_schema and declares a ROLE dictionary containing id, label, description, prompt_template, and output_schema.
observations: The module docstring records that this role was moved from ops/dragnet_extract._fine_role() to a first-class registry row during the unify-exercise 2026-06-26.
It declares the output schema must be exactly contracts.dragnet_schema.Fine and notes that schema_slot is forbidden by the dragnet-family _build_role freeze door.
The prompt_template is set to the imported FINE_PROMPT constant rather than an inline string.
The role is described as NEUTRAL and only processes chunks the step-gate tags as decision, spec, or discussion.
The file contains no functions or classes; the only runtime artifact is the ROLE dictionary.
The docstring marks the file/schema as FROZEN and PROTECTED.
standouts: Registry-row file with no executable logic beyond a single ROLE constant.
Explicit frozen-schema contract and exact-match enforcement signaled in the docstring.
Migration note from an internal helper to a first-class role definition.
conventions: Uses a module-level dict constant to register a role.
Imports schema and prompt from a contracts module rather than defining them locally.
Docstring includes provenance, versioning, and build-time constraint notes.
No __all__, no runtime validation, and no side effects in the module body.
concerns: The file asserts build-time constraints (exact Fine schema, no schema_slot) that are not enforced or verified within the file itself.
The contents of Fine and FINE_PROMPT are not shown, so correctness depends on external contracts.
No local examples, tests, or runtime checks for the ROLE dictionary.
notes: This file is essentially a typed registration card for the dragnet fine-extraction role, pinning it to a frozen schema and shared prompt.
questions: What are the exact fields and validation rules in contracts.dragnet_schema.Fine?
What is the full text and versioning of FINE_PROMPT?
How does the _build_role freeze door enforce exact output_schema and forbid schema_slot?
What determines which chunks are marked as decision|spec|discussion by the step-gate?
What does the broader dragnet bake pipeline look like beyond stage-2?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to register the neutral dragnet stage-2 fine extraction role, and the file does exactly that by declaring the ROLE dictionary with the Fine schema and FINE_PROMPT.
apparent_themes: Frozen contracts and build-time compatibility enforcement.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to make the fine extraction role a stable, discoverable configuration object that can be loaded by a builder/runtime, while locking it to a specific frozen schema and prompt to prevent drift.
novelty: It treats a role definition as a data record rather than code, and it documents build-door constraints directly in the module docstring.
contribution: It contributes the metadata binding that identifies the dragnet stage-2 fine extraction role, its intended chunk filter, its output schema, and its prompt reference, which a fused system would keep as the authoritative role record.
summary_for_embedding: roles/dragnet_fine.py defines the first-class registry row for the neutral dragnet stage-2 fine extraction role, binding the frozen Fine output schema and the shared FINE_PROMPT.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to replace an inline helper with a stable, schema-locked registry entry that a build step can validate for exact schema compatibility.
